title: tripolium subulatum
intro: Tripolium subulatum, also known as rush-like knapweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Tripolium subulatum include treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, as well as acting as a natural diuretic and laxative. Herbal preparations of Tripolium subulatum include infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which can be used externally or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
